aiTitle: Architecture design specification for uk-pirates-queen layout system

content.thinking:
This is a design specification update that adds a new subsection (5.3.1) addressing a gap in the ghost cleanup mechanism. Let me parse what was added:

**What was added:**
- A new subsection 5.3.1 "cascade 殘留 ghost 釋放（補 §5.4「全退」之機制 · 對抗驗證後追加）"
- This addresses a gap: when a pre-existing ghost (like `[M4 real, M8 ghost]`) transitions to `→[]` (all empty) in the next round, M4 goes to "gone", but M8 ghost falls into `preFreeSlots` with no one to reuse it → **residual active=true invisible leak**, Layout still counts 1 slot.
- **Mechanism**: After `ReconcileCascade` Pass B, calculate `postReal = count(m_currentBounty[i]!==null)`:
  - `postReal === 0` → release all residual ghosts (slots with `active===true && currentBounty===null` → `node.active=false; SetContentVisible(true); ClearBaseSymbol(); m_ghostOwner=-1`)
  - `postReal >= 1` → preserve (OPEN-3). Under max-2, there won't be 2 ghosts (ghost only created when hasSurvivor, so 1 real+1 ghost is the upper limit)
- **Timing**: Release must be inside `Promise.all(outPromises).then()` (after departing items finish Out animation), **to avoid departing real's Out animation being re-centered mid-animation due to sudden Layout count drop**. If there are no outPromises in that round (theoretically postReal===0 must have departing real → outPromises not empty; defensive fallback does synchronous release).
- Note: Can merge with existing deferredEnter's `Promise.all(outPromises).then()` into the same then (deferredEnter first, then residual release; the two don't conflict: when postReal===0, deferredEnter must be empty).
- The fix closes a critical lifecycle hole where orphaned ghosts persisted invisibly when containers emptied completely, corrupting slot accounting.

content.text:
```xml
<observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Ghost Cleanup Gap Fixed for All-Items-Removed Scenario</title>
  <subtitle>Added cascade residual ghost release mechanism to prevent invisible leaks when survivor and ghost both exit</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Added section 5.3.1 to design specification addressing ghost leak gap discovered during adversarial verification</fact>
    <fact>Gap scenario: pre-existing ghost in state [M4 real, M8 ghost] transitions to [] (all empty), M8 ghost falls into preFreeSlots unreused causing active=true invisible leak with Layout still counting 1 slot</fact>
    <fact>Fix mechanism: ReconcileCascade Pass B calculates postReal = count(m_currentBounty[i]!==null) after all transitions complete</fact>
    <fact>When postReal === 0, release all residual ghosts (active===true && currentBounty===null slots set to node.active=false, SetContentVisible(true), ClearBaseSymbol(), m_ghostOwner=-1)</fact>
    <fact>When postReal >= 1, preserve ghosts per OPEN-3 design intent (max-2 constraint means at most 1 real + 1 ghost)</fact>
    <fact>Release timing must occur inside Promise.all(outPromises).then() after Out animations complete to avoid re-centering departing items mid-animation due to Layout count drop</fact>
    <fact>Cleanup can merge with existing deferredEnter Promise.all(outPromises).then() block as deferredEnter is empty when postReal===0</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>A critical gap in the ghost slot lifecycle management was identified and fixed in the design specification. The original design correctly preserved ghosts during OPEN-3 scenarios (survivor continues with ghost retained for offset positioning) but failed to clean up ghosts when all items exit. In the scenario where [M4 real, M8 ghost] transitions to [] (complete exit), M4 properly goes to "gone" state, but M8 ghost falls into the preFreeSlots array with no new items to reuse it, resulting in an invisible leak where node.active=true but the item is hidden and Layout incorrectly counts 1 slot. The fix adds a postReal check after ReconcileCascade Pass B completes: if no real items remain (postReal === 0), all residual ghosts are released; otherwise they're preserved per OPEN-3. Critical timing constraint: ghost release must happen inside Promise.all(outPromises).then() after departure animations complete, otherwise the sudden Layout count drop would re-center departing items mid-animation. This can be merged with the existing deferredEnter cleanup since both use the same Promise callback and deferredEnter is guaranteed empty when all items exit.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>.claudedocs/WantedPoster-單張不置中-設計規格.md</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```

content.thinking:
The user is adding a new property to WantedPosterItem.ts. This is adding `m_contentNode: Node` with a tooltip explaining it's for ghost slot functionality - when a ghost occupies a slot, this content node is turned off so content is invisible but the item root is still counted by cc.Layout for positioning.

This is implementing part of the ghost slot design specification that was previously documented.

content.text:
```xml
<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Content Node Property Added to WantedPosterItem for Ghost Slot Control</title>
  <subtitle>Added m_contentNode property to enable hiding item content while keeping root active for Layout positioning</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Added @property m_contentNode: Node to WantedPosterItem.ts class properties section</fact>
    <fact>Tooltip documents purpose: "內容容器（Spine + 倍率）。ghost 佔位時關閉此節點：內容隱形但 item root 仍被 cc.Layout 計入版位" (Content container for Spine + multiplier; when ghost occupies slot, close this node so content is invisible but item root still counted by cc.Layout for positioning)</fact>
    <fact>Property is private and typed as Node with null default value, consistent with other node references in the class</fact>
    <fact>Implements ghost slot dual-active architecture: item root controls Layout slot occupancy, m_contentNode controls content visibility</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The WantedPosterItem component now has a content node property that implements the ghost slot architecture designed in the specification. This property will reference a new Content container node in the prefab hierarchy that wraps the Spine skeleton and multiplier label elements. The dual-active ghost slot mechanism works by keeping the item root node active (so cc.Layout counts it as occupying a grid slot) while toggling the m_contentNode's active state to control content visibility. When a wanted poster item becomes a ghost (2→1 transition where one item departs but the survivor should stay offset), the root remains active=true to preserve the Layout's 2-slot calculation, while m_contentNode.active=false hides the actual Spine and multiplier content. This prevents the Layout's CONTAINER resize mode from re-centering the survivor item. The property declaration sets up the infrastructure for the SetContentVisible() API and the ghost lifecycle management described in the design specification.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>why-it-exists</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>assets/Script/WantedPoster/WantedPosterItem.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```

<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Ghost Slot API Methods Implemented in WantedPosterItem</title>
  <subtitle>Added SetContentVisible and ClearBaseSymbol public methods to control ghost slot visibility and prevent dirty symbol reads</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Added public SetContentVisible(visible: boolean) method that sets m_contentNode.active, hiding Spine and multiplier content while item root remains active for Layout calculation</fact>
    <fact>SetContentVisible includes error handling: warns when m_contentNode is null but does not throw exception, allowing graceful degradation</fact>
    <fact>Added public ClearBaseSymbol() method that resets m_baseSymbol to Symbol.MAX, preventing GetBaseSymbol() from returning stale symbol values after ghost conversion</fact>
    <fact>ClearBaseSymbol addresses D3 defect from adversarial verification: m_baseSymbol has no clear point in original code (WantedPosterItem.ts:74/147 set values only), causing ghost items to return dirty old symbols</fact>
    <fact>TypeScript compilation passes with only moduleResolution deprecation warning (TS5107), confirming type correctness of new methods</fact>
    <fact>Both methods implement design specification sections BC-G5 and BC-G8 for dual-truth data model alignment</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The ghost slot API layer is now implemented in WantedPosterItem with two critical public methods. SetContentVisible() controls the content node's active state, implementing the dual-active architecture where the item root stays active (for cc.Layout slot counting) while the content can be hidden (ghost state) or shown (real state). The method includes defensive error handling that warns but doesn't throw when m_contentNode is unset, allowing the system to continue functioning. ClearBaseSymbol() solves a critical defect identified during adversarial verification (D3): the m_baseSymbol field was set in SetWantedPosterData and SetSkinBySymbol but never cleared, causing ghost items to return stale symbol values when GetBaseSymbol() was called. By resetting to Symbol.MAX during ghost conversion, the system prevents GetItemsByBaseSymbol from incorrectly matching ghost items with their old symbols. These methods complete the item-level API contract needed by WantedPosterManager to orchestrate ghost slot lifecycle, corresponding to BC-G5 (currentBounty-based filtering) and BC-G8 (baseSymbol clearing) behavior contracts in the design specification. TypeScript compilation confirms the implementations are type-safe.</narrative>
